message.content: Adversaries may exploit hybrid identity authentication processes to gain persistent access to cloud resources by modifying or backdooring these processes. This technique targets environments where user identities are synchronized between on-premises and cloud systems, such as those using Azure AD or AD FS. By compromising on-premises servers or cloud accounts, attackers can inject malicious DLLs or alter configuration files to bypass authentication mechanisms, capture credentials, and maintain privileged access. For instance, injecting a DLL into the AzureADConnectAuthenticationAgentService or editing the Microsoft.IdentityServer.Servicehost file in AD FS can allow unauthorized access and token generation. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and securing synchronization processes, implementing robust access controls, regularly auditing configurations, and employing multi-factor authentication to reduce the risk of unauthorized modifications.
